what_it_does: React implementation of the Tabs component. Renders a div with role='tablist' containing a button (role='tab') per item, applying .cv-tabs/.cv-tab token classes. Resolves each item's id/label from either a string or {id,label} object, marks one tab active, and wires onClick to onChange(id, e).
observations: Uses React.createElement aliased as h (no JSX syntax despite the .jsx extension)
Relies on a global React (/* global React */ comment, no import)
id resolution: object → it.id ?? it.label, otherwise the string itself; label resolution mirrors this
Active logic: if value is provided, active = (value === id); otherwise the FIRST item (i === 0) is active by default
onClick is only attached when onChange is provided (undefined otherwise)
Accessibility: role=tablist/tab and aria-selected are set
Passes through ...rest props onto the wrapper div and merges className with 'cv-tabs'
standouts: Default-active-first-tab fallback (i === 0) when uncontrolled — visual selection without state
Styling delegated entirely to .cv-tabs/.cv-tab classes, keeping the component logic-only (token-class wrapper)
conventions: Follows the design system's 'token-class wrapper' convention — no literals, styling via cv- classes
Uses h = React.createElement instead of JSX, a pattern allowing the file to run without a build/transpile step
concerns: Uncontrolled mode only ever highlights the first tab visually (i===0); clicking other tabs fires onChange but the component itself never re-renders selection without external state
notes: Logic-only data-driven tab bar: maps items to role=tab buttons, styling via cv- classes, controlled by value/onChange with first-tab-active fallback.
questions: Is the uncontrolled (no value) mode intended only as a static render, given it can't track clicks itself?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the header comment ('data-driven tab bar, token-class wrapper, controlled via value+onChange') accurately describes the implementation.
intention_signals: Trying to provide a minimal, styling-free tab primitive whose look comes entirely from design-system classes, runnable without a transpiler, and driven by data.
novelty: Build-free .jsx using React.createElement with a global React rather than imports/JSX.
contribution: Contributes the runtime behavior for the Tabs primitive — item normalization (string|object), aria-tab semantics, and the value/onChange controlled wiring that a fused system would keep.
summary_for_embedding: React Tabs component built with React.createElement that maps string-or-object items to role=tab buttons styled by cv- classes, controlled via value/onChange.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a styling-free, build-free, data-driven, accessible tab bar primitive for the design system.
